title: 10 best herbal creams for fever blister on lip

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree creams helps with fever blister on lip because of its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredient, tea tree oil, has been shown to reduce the severity and duration of fever blisters by targeting the herpes simplex virus that causes them. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing redness and swelling. As a result, tea tree creams can provide fast and effective relief from the discomfort and pain associated with fever blisters on the lip.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Tea tree oil's antiviral properties help combat the herpes simplex virus, which causes fever blisters, by interfering with its replication and reducing the severity and duration of outbreaks.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Tea tree cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with fever blisters, making them less uncomfortable and noticeable.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of tea tree oil help prevent bacterial infections that can occur when the skin is broken or scratched due to fever blister lesions, promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antiviral and antibacterial properties, tea tree leaves are effective in reducing the severity and duration of fever blisters.
Barks: The barks of the tea tree contain cineole, a compound that helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, making it ideal for treating lip sores.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize clean glass jars and utensils in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax pellets 15 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of shea butter.
Infuse one teaspoon of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for four hours.
Strain the infused oil and add it to the beeswax mixture heating slowly while stirring until melted.
Pour the melted mixture into the sterilized jars and allow it to cool and harden completely in a few hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with fever blister on lip because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as calendula oil, soothe and calm the affected area, reducing redness and swelling associated with fever blisters. Additionally, pot marigold's antimicrobial properties help to combat viral infections that cause fever blisters, promoting faster healing and reducing the duration of the outbreak.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold creams contain calendic acid, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with fever blisters, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antiviral: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in pot marigold have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, including herpes simplex virus (HSV), which causes fever blisters, reducing the severity and duration of outbreaks.

Wound healing: Calendula extract in pot marigold creams has been found to accelerate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue regeneration, and enhancing skin elasticity, making it an effective treatment for lip lesions and cracks caused by fever blisters.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and heal the affected area.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antiviral and antibacterial activities, making them effective in combating fever blister infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried calendula flowers and combine 2 tablespoons with 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture by placing it in a saucepan, covering it with a lid, and simmering for 30-40 minutes over low heat.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Mix the strained liquid with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a small saucepan, heating until melted and smooth.
Remove from heat, let cool slightly, and pour the mixture into a clean lip balm tube or tin container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with fever blister on lip because of its natural soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel-like substance in aloe vera has been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores and fever blisters. It also contains compounds that can help accelerate the healing process by promoting collagen synthesis and improving skin elasticity. As a result, applying aloe cream to affected lips can provide quick relief from discomfort and promote faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Herbal aloe creams contain compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses that cause herpes simplex, reducing the severity and duration of fever blisters.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as saponins and anthraquinones, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with fever blisters, promoting faster healing and comfort.

Wound-healing properties: Aloe vera's gel contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as amino acids and other nutrients that promote collagen synthesis, tissue repair, and wound closure, helping to speed up the recovery process of fever blisters on the lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, leaves help soothe and reduce swelling associated with fever blisters.
Buds: Rich in antimicrobial properties, buds aid in preventing infection and promoting healing of fever blister sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from mature leaves with sharp edges.
Combine aloe vera gel with cooled coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler.
Infuse the mixture with calming herbs including calendula and chamomile flowers.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl.
Whip the cooled mixture with olive oil and vitamin e oil until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with fever blisters on lip because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Antimicrobial property: Chamomile's antimicrobial properties can help combat the herpes simplex virus (HSV) that causes fever blisters, reducing the risk of infection spreading and promoting healing.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried matricaria chamomilla flowers according to your supplier's instructions or grow them in your garden.
Combine one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla with two tablespoons of coconut oil and four tablespoons of beeswax pellets.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 15-20 minutes until the beeswax is completely melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric whisk.
Transfer the cream to small glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to two weeks, shaking well before use as needed.

remedy_recipe: Infuse dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in sweet almond oil for 2 weeks or longer.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer to clean glass jars.
Combine the infused oil with beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler set at medium heat.
Allow the mixture to melt and mix well then remove from heat and let cool slightly for 10 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into small containers such as lip balm tubes or tin pots and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with fever blister on lip because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This ingredient reduces swelling and redness associated with cold sores, allowing for faster healing. remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and grind it into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 40 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and melt the mixture over medium heat for 15 minutes.
Add 30 grams of ground glycyrrhiza glabra root to the melted wax-oil mixture and stir well to combine, then let it steep for 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and add 20 drops of tea tree essential oil and 10 drops of lavender essential oil, stirring until well combined; let cool until solidified for 30 minutes.
Once cooled, pour the cream into a sterilized container and store in the refrigerator at 40°f to 50°f (4°c to 10°c) for up to 2 weeks, using as needed to soothe fever blisters on the lip.

remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including echinacea purpurea flowers 20% by weight, sweet almond oil 60% by weight, and beeswax 20% by weight.
Dry the echinacea purpurea flowers in a warm oven at 150°f for two hours to enhance their medicinal properties.
Combine one part of dried echinacea purpurea flowers with two parts of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids, and add beeswax to achieve a 20% concentration.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for one hour before whipping it with a hand mixer for five minutes.

Combine 2 tablespoons of the cooled infusion with 4 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried plantago major leaves and stems from a reputable source or your own garden.
Mix the plant material with 40 grams of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar for 2 hours.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 60-70°c for 4 hours to infuse the oil with plantago major's active compounds.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth into another clean glass jar and discard the plant material leaving 30 grams of infused oil.
Combine the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and melt at 60-70°c for 15 minutes to create a smooth, creamy texture.

remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including dried symphytum officinale herb, coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin e oil in sufficient quantities for the desired batch size.
Combine one tablespoon of dried symphytum officinale herb with two tablespoons of boiling water and steep for 10 to 15 minutes to create a herbal infusion.
Strain the herbal infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids, and let it cool to room temperature.
Mix the cooled herbal infusion with one tablespoon of melted coconut oil, two tablespoons of melted beeswax, and one teaspoon of vitamin e oil in a heat-resistant bowl until well combined.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to six months or use within three weeks at room temperature.

Combine the powdered euphorbia peplus with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a clean glass jar, stirring well for 5 minutes.

Combine one part dried hypericum perforatum herb with four parts coconut oil or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm dark place for 2-4 weeks shaking the jar daily to release oils and active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids and herbs.
Whip the remaining oil solution with an electric mixer until it thickens to a creamy consistency taking approximately 10 minutes.

Add 5 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil to the strained rosa rubiginosa oil and heat the mixture gently until fully incorporated.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of dried hamamelis virginiana bark and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground hamamelis virginiana powder with 20 grams of beeswax, 30 grams of coconut oil, and 10 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler for 15 minutes.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 1 hour, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f, ensuring complete melting.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature while whisking it every 5 minutes to prevent separation for 20 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator at a temperature of 39°f to 45°f for up to 6 months.

Grind 1 cup of the dried berries into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered berries with 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.

Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 100°f before pouring it into sterilized lip balm tubes or containers for storage at room temperature.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in white willow cream reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with fever blusters, promoting a faster healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in salicin, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and reduce swelling associated with fever blisters.

Barks: Containing a high concentration of salicin, the barks of White Willow have been used for centuries to create topical creams that alleviate pain, itching, and inflammation caused by fever blisters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salix alba bark and grind it into a fine powder weighing approximately 50 grams.
Combine the powdered salix alba bark with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed extra virgin olive oil and stir for 10 minutes.
Add 20 drops of tea tree essential oil and 10 drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture and blend thoroughly.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler set at medium heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool completely before transferring it to sterilized glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with fever blisters on the lip because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The thymol in thyme has been shown to combat the herpes simplex virus that causes fever blisters, reducing inflammation and accelerating healing. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties help prevent secondary infections, promoting a faster recovery from outbreaks. As a result, using thyme creams can provide relief from painful and unsightly fever blisters on the lip.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Thyme cream helps to combat bacterial infections that can cause and exacerbate fever blisters, reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: Thyme cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol, help to reduce swelling, redness, and discomfort associated with fever blisters, promoting a faster recovery.

Antiviral Properties: Thyme cream's antiviral properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the replication of herpes simplex virus (HSV), help to suppress the viral activity that causes fever blisters, reducing their frequency and severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and combat the viral causes of fever blisters, promoting healing and soothing lip irritation.
Buds: Thyme buds contain antioxidants and flavonoids that provide anti-inflammatory relief, reducing swelling and redness associated with fever blisters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves measure out 10 grams of dried thyme flowers and grind them into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Combine ground thyme with 20 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat mixture gently stirring occasionally over low heat.
Allow the mixture to steep for 30 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 65 degrees celsius then remove from heat and let cool.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar discarding the solids and store the liquid in an airtight container at room temperature.

Mix 10 milliliters of the thyme-infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of coconut oil in a small saucepan heating until the beeswax melts then pour into lip balm tubes or tin containers.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for fever blister on lip are natural topical remedies that utilize the healing properties of plants to soothe and calm the discomfort caused by fever blisters. These creams work by reducing inflammation, easing pain, and accelerating the healing process. Examples of herbal creams that help with fever blisters include tea tree oil cream, aloe vera gel, and calendula cream. By using these natural remedies, individuals can reduce the duration and severity of their fever blister outbreaks, improving their overall quality of life and confidence in social situations.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat fever blister on lip with herbal creams is by using a combination of soothing and anti-inflammatory herbs. Look for creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile, which help calm the skin and reduce swelling. Apply the cream several times a day to keep the area moist and promote healing. Additionally, consider mixing a small amount of honey with the cream for its antimicrobial properties.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to fever blister on lip that are treated with herbal creams are cold sores, herpes simplex, and canker sores. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and lavender oil have anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties that help soothe and heal these conditions. These natural remedies may reduce symptoms such as pain, swelling, and inflammation, promoting faster healing and preventing scarring.
